name: Shatrughna
description: Shatrughna, whose name translates to 'destroyer of enemies,' is a significant figure in Hindu mythology, most prominently known as the youngest of the four sons of King Dasharatha and Queen Sumitra of Ayodhya in the epic Ramayana. He is considered an incarnation of a portion of Vishnu, who incarnated as Rama. Shatrughna is particularly revered for his unwavering loyalty and devotion to his elder brother, Bharata. While Rama embodies righteousness and Lakshmana represents unwavering service, Shatrughna exemplifies selfless dedication and the quiet strength of a steadfast supporter. He is not often at the forefront of the major events in the Ramayana, yet his actions, particularly his slaying of the demon Lavanasura, highlight his prowess and importance in upholding dharma. His character serves as an embodiment of devotion and righteous action, emphasizing the importance of loyalty and support within familial and societal structures. Shatrughna's narrative underscores the multifaceted nature of heroism and the significance of seemingly secondary roles in maintaining cosmic balance.
subtitle: Destroyer of Enemies

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of Lavanasura
keyMyths.description: Shatrughna's most notable feat is the slaying of the demon Lavanasura. This demon terrorized the land with his trident, which was granted to him by Lord Shiva. Rama entrusted Shatrughna with the task of killing Lavanasura and restoring peace. Shatrughna successfully vanquished the demon after a fierce battle, wielding Vishnu's Sudarshana Chakra. He then established the city of Mathura on the site of Lavanasura's defeat, marking a significant victory for dharma and establishing Shatrughna as a protector of the innocent.

keyMyths.title: Shatrughna's Devotion to Bharata
keyMyths.description: During Rama's exile, Bharata ruled Ayodhya as a regent, refusing to sit on the throne and instead placing Rama's sandals there as a symbol of his absent brother's authority. Shatrughna remained steadfastly by Bharata's side, supporting his brother's unwavering dedication to Rama and his commitment to upholding dharma. This illustrates the deep bond between the brothers and Shatrughna's unwavering loyalty to his family.

keyMyths.title: Shatrughna's Role in Rama's Ashwamedha Yagna
keyMyths.description: After Rama's return to Ayodhya and his coronation, he performed the Ashwamedha Yagna, a ritual to assert his sovereignty. Shatrughna was entrusted with the responsibility of protecting the sacrificial horse, showcasing his valor and his important role in maintaining the sanctity of the yagna. He ensured the successful completion of the ritual, contributing to the establishment of Rama's righteous rule.

sources.author: Valmiki
